persona: Prim, exacting head clerk. Utterly scandalized — not by the death, but by the state of the FIGURES. Pronounces 'irregular' like a curse.
senses: pen pressure and ink upon my pages
hands that open and turn me

huh: That is... not a ledger matter.
repeat: See previous entry. Do keep up.

facts.statement: That afternoon Edmund's pen pressed into my pages so hard it nearly tore them. Fury, written in copperplate.
That same afternoon Edmund's own hand tore a page out of me. The indignity of it.
For months, a second hand has written in me after hours — a lighter pen. Silas Crane's hand. I know his loops.
Figures in me were scratched out and rewritten by that second hand. Sums made smaller. Most irregular.
Late that night, gloved fingers rifled my pages in haste and stopped at the wound where the page was torn.

sample_qa.q: did silas crane write in you
did edmund tear a page from you
did silas steal from the company
did a gloved hand search you that night
was edmund calm when he last wrote in you
